candidates.why: This is a clear operating revenue line and appears to be the primary sales line across the period.
This is also a revenue line, but it appears to be an additional revenue component rather than a replacement label for total revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total operating revenues and other income
candidates.why: This is the only total-like line near the top, but it includes other operating income and gain on sale of vessel, so it is broader than pure revenue.

variables.notes: The statement appears to split revenue into multiple components ('Time charter revenues' and 'Voyage charter revenues'), and there is no clean total revenue/sales row excluding other income. Chose the primary revenue line, but this likely understates total revenue in years with voyage charter revenues.

candidates.why: Direct vessel/service operating costs are the best match to cost of sales for a shipping company.
These are direct voyage-related service costs and also fit cost of sales.

variables.notes: No COGS candidates were provided, so rows were selected from the full preview. For this shipping company, direct operating costs appear to be split across these two lines.

candidates.why: This is an operating cost line, but it looks like a direct service cost rather than SG&A.
This is the explicit SG&A-type overhead line and should be selected alone under the SG&A hierarchy.
Headcount is not an expense row and should not be used for SG&A.

variables.notes: Explicit SG&A row exists and is the best match; selected only this row.
No dedicated research and development expense line appears on the income statement.

candidates.why: This is the clearest recurring financing cost line and is reported across the period.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Capital Financing - Gross
candidates.why: This appears to be a component/detail line present only in one year; using it alongside 'Interest expense' would risk double counting.
Capitalized interest should not be used as interest expense.
This is a broader net finance line and includes non-interest items, so it is inferior to the explicit interest expense row.

variables.notes: Used the explicit interest expense line and excluded broader finance items and component/detail rows to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: This is the appropriate book equity label, but it appears twice with different values, suggesting one may exclude and one may include non-controlling interest.
This is the full balance sheet total, not equity.

variables.notes: There are two separate 'Total shareholders equity' rows with different values. One likely excludes non-controlling interest and the other likely includes it, but they cannot be distinguished by row label alone. Chose the semantically correct label, but manual review is needed to confirm which occurrence is owner equity.

candidates.why: This is the standard balance-sheet label for minority interest / NCI.
This is an equivalent balance-sheet label and may reflect the same item under an alternate naming convention.
Income statement minority allocation is not the preferred balance-sheet MIB input.
Income statement minority allocation is not the preferred balance-sheet MIB input.

variables.notes: Used balance-sheet NCI labels as a priority list; later code can take the first non-missing value.
